stories.headline: April crypto hacks crack $620M after Kelp DAO and Drift back-to-back drains
stories.tldr: April losses from crypto exploits topped $620M across 12 incidents, the worst single month since February 2025 and 3x Q1 combined.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Cross-chain bridges and admin keys are bleeding faster than insurance funds, freezing DeFi blue chips and validating institutional reluctance to hold protocol risk on-chain.
the_details: Kelp DAO lost $292M on April 19 after an attacker drained 116,500 rsETH (~18% of supply) via the LayerZero bridge.
Drift Protocol shed $285M on April 1 in a North Korea-linked social-engineering attack using pre-signed authorizations.
Aave, SparkLend, Fluid, Upshift and CoW Swap all triggered emergency freezes during the Kelp aftermath.

stories.headline: MegaETH token generation event lands today after years of fast-L2 hype
stories.tldr: MegaETH's MEGA token launches today, finally putting a price on Ethereum's most-anticipated real-time Layer 2.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: A live MEGA market lets the market re-rate the entire fast-L2 thesis — Base, Arbitrum and Optimism now compete with a chain pitching 100k TPS sub-10ms blocks.
the_details: TGE date confirmed for April 30, 2026 in CoinDesk's Crypto Week Ahead schedule.
MegaETH targets 100,000 TPS with 10ms block times, positioning as the fastest Ethereum L2 in production.
Listing flow will test whether 2026's altcoin season index — currently 33-38/100 — has any capacity for new high-FDV launches.

stories.headline: Five-front macro pile-on hits today: FOMC, ECB, BoE, US Q1 GDP and PCE
stories.tldr: Crypto faces the densest macro print of the quarter today as Fed, ECB and BoE all decide rates while US Q1 GDP and March PCE drop in the same window.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Three central banks plus two top-tier US data prints means BTC's compressed implied vol gets one shot to break — directionally, the move sets the tone for May.
the_details: Bank of England and ECB rate decisions, US Q1 GDP, March PCE Price Index and Initial Jobless Claims all land April 30.
BTC enters the day pinned near $77,794 with spot volume below $8B, the thinnest tape since October 2023.
Spot Bitcoin ETFs absorbed roughly $1.7B in April, the largest monthly haul since October 2025.

stories.headline: Meta pays creators in stablecoins via Stripe as Visa rail hits $7B run rate
stories.tldr: Meta is now paying select creators in stablecoins through Stripe while Visa's stablecoin settlement network reaches a $7B annualized volume.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Stablecoins just crossed from crypto rail into mainstream payouts — Web2 distribution at this scale is what bank-issued stablecoins under the GENIUS Act were built for.
the_details: Meta's creator payout pilot uses Stripe-issued stablecoins, expanding beyond bank-only disbursements.
Visa's stablecoin settlement volume now runs at roughly $7B annualized after recent corridor expansion.
FDIC opened GENIUS Act stablecoin rulemaking in April, putting bank issuers on a clearer compliance path.

stories.headline: Western Union to launch USDPT stablecoin on Solana as May settlement rail
stories.tldr: Western Union confirmed a May 2026 launch for USDPT, a US-dollar stablecoin built on Solana for institutional cross-border settlement.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: A 170-year-old remittance giant choosing Solana over Ethereum or a private chain hands SOL its strongest TradFi distribution proof point yet.
the_details: USDPT will go live in May 2026 as Western Union's institutional settlement token.
Solana hits the launch with Alpenglow consensus rolling out, cutting confirmation times further.
SOL trades near $85.97 with the altcoin season index at 33-38/100, leaving headroom if the WU news lands.
